channel.title: Home on Performance 2026
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Performance 2026

item.title: Call for Papers
item.description:
OVERVIEW

The IFIP Performance 2026 conference will be held in Ghent, Belgium from 3 to 5 November 2026, and aims to bring together researchers interested in understanding and improving the performance of computing and communication systems using state-of-the-art quantitative models and solution techniques.

We invite submissions of research papers focusing on algorithm design, mathematical analysis and modeling, simulation, and measurement techniques for computer systems and communication networks. We particularly welcome contributions that introduce novel performance evaluation methods or creatively apply existing techniques to predict or gain insights into key design trade-offs across a wide range of computing, networked, and cyber-physical systems. These include mobile devices, Internet-of-Things (IoT) applications, and large-scale cloud computing environments. Submissions of reproducible experiments are also encouraged.

item.title: Call for Workshops
item.description:
Call for Workshops

PERFORMANCE 2026 is soliciting proposals for one-day workshops to be held in conjunction with the conference. Workshops will take place on Thursday, 5 November 2026.

We invite you to submit workshop proposals on any topic relevant to the performance community. Topics include, but are not limited to, mathematical modelling techniques and formalisms, data-driven optimisation, machine learning and data analytics, smart grids, social networks and web services, blockchain, energy-efficient computing, quantum computing, and cyber security.

item.title: Organisation

Organisation

General chair
Dieter Fiems, Ghent University

TPC chairs
Esa Hyytiä, University of Iceland
Michela Meo, Politecnico di Torino

Koen De Turck, Ghent University

Alessandro Zocca (Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam)
Martin Zubeldia (University of Minnesota)\

item.title: Venue
item.description:
Venue

City of Ghent

Located in the heart of Flanders, Ghent is one of Belgium’s most enchanting and authentic cities — a place where the echoes of the Middle Ages meet the creativity and energy of the present. Its compact centre invites exploration on foot, with every turn revealing another postcard-perfect view. The city’s skyline is defined by the three towers of Ghent — Saint Nicholas’ Church, the Belfry, and Saint Bavo’s Cathedral — which stand proudly in line and symbolise Ghent’s rich spiritual, civic, and artistic heritage. Inside Saint Bavo’s, visitors can admire the world-renowned Adoration of the Mystic Lamb, a 15th-century altarpiece by the Van Eyck brothers that remains one of Europe’s greatest artistic treasures.
